raw_response:
{
  "communicative_function": "Asserting a philosophical principle of radical self-responsibility and personal agency in shaping one's character and life",
  "implicit_claims": [
    "Individuals possess sufficient volitional control to determine their own identity and circumstances",
    "External conditions do not ultimately dictate one's nature or destiny",
    "Self-formation occurs through internal processes such as thought and will"
  ],
  "coherence_assessment": "Internally coherent as an emphatic, near-tautological affirmation of self-causation, though it risks circularity without further elaboration on mechanisms",
  "conditions_for_usefulness": "Individuals must have meaningful agency, access to resources for self-change, and a context where emphasizing personal responsibility produces constructive motivation rather than despair",
  "conditions_for_misleading": "When external constraints (systemic inequality, trauma, genetic factors) heavily limit agency, rendering the claim an oversimplification that promotes undue self-blame"
}
